course.title: CompTIA Network+ N10-009 Applied Networking
course.description: Entry-level network technicians and support professionals who need vendor-neutral N10-009 knowledge plus repeated practical design, command, capture, security, operations, and troubleshooting experience. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate all comptia network plus n10-009 objectives and domain weights through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate vendor-neutral performance-based configuration, packet, design, security, and troubleshooting practice through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate four cumulative branch, wireless, operations, and certification projects through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.
misconceptions: Trying the most common fix before recording symptoms is the fastest reliable method.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.

competencies.statement: Separate user report, observed symptom, affected scope, recent change, and reproducible condition before forming a theory.
misconceptions: A user description of the cause is equivalent to independently observed evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate user report, observed symptom, affected scope, recent change, and reproducible condition before forming a theory.

competencies.statement: Form one falsifiable probable-cause theory and isolate it with bottom-up, top-down, or divide-and-conquer evidence.
misconceptions: Changing several layers together is efficient because one change will reveal the cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Form one falsifiable probable-cause theory and isolate it with bottom-up, top-down, or divide-and-conquer evidence.

competencies.statement: Assess impact, backup configuration, define rollback, obtain authority, and avoid destructive testing on live networks.
misconceptions: Troubleshooting authority automatically includes permission to change production configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assess impact, backup configuration, define rollback, obtain authority, and avoid destructive testing on live networks.

competencies.statement: Verify the original task plus retained services and identify a preventive control after a bounded repair.
misconceptions: A successful ping proves the full affected application and user workflow is restored.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify the original task plus retained services and identify a preventive control after a bounded repair.

competencies.statement: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.
misconceptions: Command history alone is a complete and understandable incident record.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.

competencies.statement: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
misconceptions: Every protocol and appliance operates at exactly one OSI layer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.

competencies.statement: Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
misconceptions: OSI layers five through seven always appear as separate headers on the wire.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.

competencies.statement: Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
misconceptions: A router forwards the original Ethernet frame unchanged between networks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish MAC, IP, port, name, and application identifiers by scope, lifetime, and forwarding purpose.
misconceptions: A destination MAC address identifies the final remote server across the internet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish MAC, IP, port, name, and application identifiers by scope, lifetime, and forwarding purpose.

competencies.statement: Compare TCP reliability and state with UDP datagrams and select transport behavior from application requirements.
misconceptions: UDP is always faster and TCP is always more appropriate for important data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare TCP reliability and state with UDP datagrams and select transport behavior from application requirements.

competencies.statement: Interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.
misconceptions: One packet capture contains every hop and decrypted application payload automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.

competencies.statement: Compare router, switch, firewall, access point, controller, proxy, load balancer, IDS, and IPS forwarding or inspection roles.
misconceptions: A device name determines one fixed OSI layer and one fixed deployment form.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare router, switch, firewall, access point, controller, proxy, load balancer, IDS, and IPS forwarding or inspection roles.

competencies.statement: Compare NAS and SAN access models, protocols, traffic patterns, redundancy, and use cases.
misconceptions: NAS and SAN are interchangeable names for any shared disk appliance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare NAS and SAN access models, protocols, traffic patterns, redundancy, and use cases.

competencies.statement: Explain CDN, VPN, QoS, and TTL functions and how each changes delivery, privacy, priority, or loop lifetime.
misconceptions: QoS creates bandwidth and guarantees performance across networks you do not control.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain CDN, VPN, QoS, and TTL functions and how each changes delivery, privacy, priority, or loop lifetime.

competencies.statement: Explain NFV, VPC boundaries, security groups, security lists, subnets, route tables, and virtual gateways.
misconceptions: A VPC is physically isolated hardware and therefore needs no network policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain NFV, VPC boundaries, security groups, security lists, subnets, route tables, and virtual gateways.

competencies.statement: Choose internet, NAT, VPN, or dedicated cloud connectivity by direction, reachability, performance, and risk.
misconceptions: A NAT gateway accepts unsolicited inbound internet connections by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose internet, NAT, VPN, or dedicated cloud connectivity by direction, reachability, performance, and risk.

competencies.statement: Compare public, private, hybrid and SaaS, PaaS, IaaS responsibility boundaries.
misconceptions: Moving to IaaS transfers operating-system and network security responsibility to the provider.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare public, private, hybrid and SaaS, PaaS, IaaS responsibility boundaries.

competencies.statement: Distinguish scalability, elasticity, multitenancy, availability, and geographic placement with measurable requirements.
misconceptions: Elasticity and scalability are identical terms for buying a larger server.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish scalability, elasticity, multitenancy, availability, and geographic placement with measurable requirements.

competencies.statement: Use N10-009 standard ports for file transfer, remote administration, mail, DNS, DHCP, web, time, monitoring, directory, database, desktop, and voice services.
misconceptions: Knowing a port number proves the service is enabled, secure, and reachable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use N10-009 standard ports for file transfer, remote administration, mail, DNS, DHCP, web, time, monitoring, directory, database, desktop, and voice services.

competencies.statement: Compare secure and insecure service alternatives such as SSH or Telnet, HTTPS or HTTP, LDAPS or LDAP, and SFTP or FTP.
misconceptions: Changing a service to a commonly secure port automatically enables encryption.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare secure and insecure service alternatives such as SSH or Telnet, HTTPS or HTTP, LDAPS or LDAP, and SFTP or FTP.

competencies.statement: Explain how DNS, DHCP, NTP, PTP, and NTS dependencies affect authentication, addressing, logging, and service reachability.
misconceptions: Successful IP connectivity proves DNS and time are irrelevant to the application.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how DNS, DHCP, NTP, PTP, and NTS dependencies affect authentication, addressing, logging, and service reachability.

competencies.statement: Compare ICMP, TCP, UDP, and GRE purpose, headers, state, errors, and troubleshooting evidence.
misconceptions: ICMP is a transport protocol that uses TCP-style port numbers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare ICMP, TCP, UDP, and GRE purpose, headers, state, errors, and troubleshooting evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain AH, ESP, IKE, tunnel or transport placement, encryption, integrity, peer negotiation, and NAT interaction.
misconceptions: IPsec is one encryption protocol that always protects the complete original frame.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain AH, ESP, IKE, tunnel or transport placement, encryption, integrity, peer negotiation, and NAT interaction.

competencies.statement: Distinguish unicast, broadcast, multicast, and anycast delivery scope and network behavior.
misconceptions: Anycast sends one packet simultaneously to every server sharing the address.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish unicast, broadcast, multicast, and anycast delivery scope and network behavior.

competencies.statement: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.
misconceptions: A higher cable category always increases link speed without device or distance constraints.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.

competencies.statement: Compare single-mode and multimode fiber, wavelengths, distance, bandwidth, bend, contamination, and safety concerns.
misconceptions: Single-mode and multimode fiber can share any transceiver when connectors fit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare single-mode and multimode fiber, wavelengths, distance, bandwidth, bend, contamination, and safety concerns.

competencies.statement: Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.
misconceptions: Wireless bandwidth labels describe guaranteed application throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.

competencies.statement: Identify SC, LC, ST, MPO, RJ11, RJ45, F-type, and BNC connectors and avoid incompatible media assumptions.
misconceptions: Connector shape alone identifies cable category, speed, and supported protocol.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify SC, LC, ST, MPO, RJ11, RJ45, F-type, and BNC connectors and avoid incompatible media assumptions.

competencies.statement: Select Ethernet or Fibre Channel SFP and QSFP form factors by protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, lane, and device support.
misconceptions: Any SFP with the right connector operates in any SFP port.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select Ethernet or Fibre Channel SFP and QSFP form factors by protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, lane, and device support.

competencies.statement: Compare mesh, hybrid, star, hub-and-spoke, point-to-point, and failure effects.
misconceptions: A logical topology diagram always matches the physical cable layout.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare mesh, hybrid, star, hub-and-spoke, point-to-point, and failure effects.

competencies.statement: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.
misconceptions: Spine-leaf eliminates oversubscription and all possible single points of failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.

competencies.statement: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.
misconceptions: Subnet masks may use any pattern of ones and zeros if host counts work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.

competencies.statement: Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.
misconceptions: A private IPv4 address is globally unique and internet-routable after DNS registration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.

competencies.statement: Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.
misconceptions: The first and last address are usable hosts in every IPv4 subnet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.

competencies.statement: Allocate largest-to-smallest VLSM networks with growth, infrastructure, alignment, and non-overlap evidence.
misconceptions: VLSM means choosing unrelated masks without aligning subnet boundaries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate largest-to-smallest VLSM networks with growth, infrastructure, alignment, and non-overlap evidence.

competencies.statement: Summarize contiguous IPv4 networks only when common prefix, routing ownership, and failure behavior support aggregation.
misconceptions: Any adjacent-looking decimal networks can be summarized into one route.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Summarize contiguous IPv4 networks only when common prefix, routing ownership, and failure behavior support aggregation.

competencies.statement: Recognize class A through E historical boundaries without using classful thinking to replace CIDR design.
misconceptions: Modern routing still derives subnet masks from the first IPv4 octet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize class A through E historical boundaries without using classful thinking to replace CIDR design.

competencies.statement: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.
misconceptions: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.

competencies.statement: Compress, expand, classify, and subnet IPv6 addresses and prefixes without inventing IPv4 broadcast behavior.
misconceptions: IPv6 removes subnetting because the address space is large.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compress, expand, classify, and subnet IPv6 addresses and prefixes without inventing IPv4 broadcast behavior.

competencies.statement: Distinguish global unicast, link-local, unique local, multicast, anycast, loopback, and unspecified IPv6 addresses.
misconceptions: IPv6 uses broadcast for neighbor discovery just as IPv4 uses ARP broadcast.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish global unicast, link-local, unique local, multicast, anycast, loopback, and unspecified IPv6 addresses.

competencies.statement: Explain SLAAC, router advertisements, DHCPv6 choices, neighbor discovery, and default-gateway learning.
misconceptions: SLAAC always supplies DNS and every other host option without additional mechanisms.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain SLAAC, router advertisements, DHCPv6 choices, neighbor discovery, and default-gateway learning.

competencies.statement: Choose dual stack, tunneling, or NAT64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs.
misconceptions: NAT64 allows any IPv6-only application to reach any IPv4 service transparently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose dual stack, tunneling, or NAT64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs.

competencies.statement: Explain SDN control separation, SD-WAN policy and transport abstraction, and VXLAN overlay identifiers and use cases.
misconceptions: SD-WAN replaces all underlay circuits and makes transport performance irrelevant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain SDN control separation, SD-WAN policy and transport abstraction, and VXLAN overlay identifiers and use cases.

competencies.statement: Relate zero-trust policy decisions, least privilege, continuous context, SASE, and SSE service boundaries.
misconceptions: Zero trust means no internal connectivity is allowed and a VPN is unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate zero-trust policy decisions, least privilege, continuous context, SASE, and SSE service boundaries.

competencies.statement: Manage network templates, playbooks, inventories, source control, review, conflict, drift, upgrade, and rollback evidence.
misconceptions: Automation removes the need for change review because templates are repeatable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Manage network templates, playbooks, inventories, source control, review, conflict, drift, upgrade, and rollback evidence.

competencies.statement: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
misconceptions: Routers choose the route with the lowest metric before comparing prefix length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.

competencies.statement: Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.
misconceptions: A default route overrides more specific routes because zero is the lowest network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.

competencies.statement: Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.
misconceptions: All dynamic routing protocols choose paths using the same metric.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.

competencies.statement: Distinguish prefix length, administrative distance, and protocol metric in route-selection evidence.
misconceptions: Administrative distance is advertised end to end by every routing protocol.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish prefix length, administrative distance, and protocol metric in route-selection evidence.

competencies.statement: Trace static or dynamic NAT and PAT translations, direction, state, address conservation, and troubleshooting evidence.
misconceptions: NAT is a firewall and automatically permits only safe traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace static or dynamic NAT and PAT translations, direction, state, address conservation, and troubleshooting evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain FHRP virtual IP and gateway failover behavior, election, state, and host transparency.
misconceptions: A virtual IP load-balances every client packet equally across gateways.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain FHRP virtual IP and gateway failover behavior, election, state, and host transparency.

competencies.statement: Use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1Q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.
misconceptions: One physical interface can carry only one IP network and one VLAN.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1Q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.

competencies.statement: Create VLANs and assign access interfaces while matching documentation, endpoint purpose, and broadcast-domain boundaries.
misconceptions: A VLAN exists automatically when an access port is assigned an unused number.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create VLANs and assign access interfaces while matching documentation, endpoint purpose, and broadcast-domain boundaries.

competencies.statement: Configure 802.1Q trunks, allowed VLANs, native VLAN, and voice VLAN behavior without mismatch or leakage.
misconceptions: Native VLAN traffic is always tagged and therefore cannot mismatch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure 802.1Q trunks, allowed VLANs, native VLAN, and voice VLAN behavior without mismatch or leakage.

competencies.statement: Explain switch virtual interfaces for management or Layer 3 gateways and the dependencies for an SVI to become operational.
misconceptions: Creating an SVI automatically routes between VLANs on every switch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain switch virtual interfaces for management or Layer 3 gateways and the dependencies for an SVI to become operational.

competencies.statement: Predict root bridge selection, root and designated roles, alternate paths, states, convergence, and loop symptoms.
misconceptions: Spanning tree disables every redundant link permanently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict root bridge selection, root and designated roles, alternate paths, states, convergence, and loop symptoms.

competencies.statement: Bundle compatible links with a shared aggregation protocol and verify member, hashing, capacity, and failure behavior.
misconceptions: Two physical links in a bundle double one flow throughput automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bundle compatible links with a shared aggregation protocol and verify member, hashing, capacity, and failure behavior.

competencies.statement: Negotiate or configure compatible speed and duplex and diagnose mismatch through counters and link behavior.
misconceptions: A duplex mismatch always prevents the link from coming up.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Negotiate or configure compatible speed and duplex and diagnose mismatch through counters and link behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose MTU and jumbo-frame settings end to end while accounting for encapsulation and path consistency.
misconceptions: Enabling jumbo frames on one switch improves every connected application.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose MTU and jumbo-frame settings end to end while accounting for encapsulation and path consistency.

competencies.statement: Compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 GHz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.
misconceptions: A newer Wi-Fi generation guarantees every client uses its maximum speed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 GHz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.

competencies.statement: Choose channel, width, reuse, and band steering from regulatory domain, overlap, contention, capacity, and coverage.
misconceptions: Wider channels always increase network capacity in crowded deployments.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose channel, width, reuse, and band steering from regulatory domain, overlap, contention, capacity, and coverage.

competencies.statement: Distinguish SSID, BSSID, ESS, hidden names, and client discovery without treating an SSID as a security control.
misconceptions: Hiding an SSID prevents unauthorized clients from discovering the network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish SSID, BSSID, ESS, hidden names, and client discovery without treating an SSID as a security control.

competencies.statement: Choose infrastructure, mesh, ad hoc, and point-to-point wireless topology from backhaul, mobility, scale, and failure needs.
misconceptions: Mesh access points need no wired or wireless backhaul planning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose infrastructure, mesh, ad hoc, and point-to-point wireless topology from backhaul, mobility, scale, and failure needs.

competencies.statement: Choose WPA2 or WPA3 and PSK or enterprise authentication with migration, certificate, credential, and client constraints.
misconceptions: WPA3 personal eliminates weak passwords and all evil-twin risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose WPA2 or WPA3 and PSK or enterprise authentication with migration, certificate, credential, and client constraints.

competencies.statement: Design guest isolation and captive portals without confusing web acceptance with link-layer encryption or identity.
misconceptions: A captive portal encrypts all guest wireless traffic automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design guest isolation and captive portals without confusing web acceptance with link-layer encryption or identity.

competencies.statement: Choose directional or omnidirectional antennas and autonomous or lightweight AP management from coverage and operations needs.
misconceptions: Higher antenna gain creates more transmit power in every direction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose directional or omnidirectional antennas and autonomous or lightweight AP management from coverage and operations needs.

competencies.statement: Perform predictive and measured surveys and verify RSSI, SNR, interference, utilization, roaming, and application outcomes.
misconceptions: Strong RSSI alone proves a wireless cell has sufficient capacity and quality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Perform predictive and measured surveys and verify RSSI, SNR, interference, utilization, roaming, and application outcomes.

competencies.statement: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.
misconceptions: An IDF is simply a smaller rack and needs no distance or power planning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.

competencies.statement: Document patch panels, fiber distribution panels, labels, polarity, ports, pathways, and change ownership.
misconceptions: Patch panels are active devices that regenerate weak signals.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document patch panels, fiber distribution panels, labels, polarity, ports, pathways, and change ownership.

competencies.statement: Align port-side intake or exhaust, hot and cold airflow, lockable access, and maintenance reach.
misconceptions: Mixing intake directions improves cooling by circulating more air.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align port-side intake or exhaust, hot and cold airflow, lockable access, and maintenance reach.

competencies.statement: Calculate UPS runtime, PDU and circuit load, voltage, redundancy, graceful shutdown, and PoE power budget.
misconceptions: A PDU always provides battery backup and voltage conversion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate UPS runtime, PDU and circuit load, voltage, redundancy, graceful shutdown, and PoE power budget.

competencies.statement: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
misconceptions: Any fire extinguisher is safe for energized network equipment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.

competencies.statement: Configure DHCP scopes, leases, reservations, exclusions, gateways, DNS options, and pool capacity.
misconceptions: A reservation and an exclusion are interchangeable DHCP configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure DHCP scopes, leases, reservations, exclusions, gateways, DNS options, and pool capacity.

competencies.statement: Use DHCP relay or IP helper across routed boundaries and trace discover, offer, request, and acknowledgment evidence.
misconceptions: DHCP broadcast crosses routers automatically when a server exists elsewhere.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use DHCP relay or IP helper across routed boundaries and trace discover, offer, request, and acknowledgment evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose SLAAC, stateless or stateful DHCPv6, and router-advertisement flags based on address and option needs.
misconceptions: DHCPv6 supplies the IPv6 default gateway to clients.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose SLAAC, stateless or stateful DHCPv6, and router-advertisement flags based on address and option needs.

competencies.statement: Use A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, NS, and PTR records with correct owner, target, and dependency semantics.
misconceptions: A CNAME can coexist freely with every other record at the same owner name.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, NS, and PTR records with correct owner, target, and dependency semantics.

competencies.statement: Distinguish forward and reverse, primary and secondary, authoritative and non-authoritative, recursive, caching, and hosts-file behavior.
misconceptions: A recursive resolver is authoritative for every answer it caches.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish forward and reverse, primary and secondary, authoritative and non-authoritative, recursive, caching, and hosts-file behavior.

competencies.statement: Explain DNSSEC authenticity and DoH or DoT transport privacy without claiming either provides every property.
misconceptions: DNS over HTTPS validates that a DNS answer was signed by the zone owner.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain DNSSEC authenticity and DoH or DoT transport privacy without claiming either provides every property.

competencies.statement: Choose and verify NTP, PTP, and NTS by precision, hierarchy, authentication, delay, and application requirements.
misconceptions: NTP and PTP provide identical accuracy and security on every network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and verify NTP, PTP, and NTS by precision, hierarchy, authentication, delay, and application requirements.

competencies.statement: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
misconceptions: A site-to-site VPN automatically authorizes every device on both networks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.

competencies.statement: Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
misconceptions: Full tunnel guarantees all endpoint traffic is secure even when the device is compromised.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.

competencies.statement: Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
misconceptions: A web management interface is safer than SSH because it is graphical.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.

competencies.statement: Use a hardened jump host as a controlled management choke point with MFA, recording, patching, and limited reach.
misconceptions: A jump host removes the need to secure the destination devices.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a hardened jump host as a controlled management choke point with MFA, recording, patching, and limited reach.

competencies.statement: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.
misconceptions: Out-of-band management is physically unreachable to attackers by definition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.

competencies.statement: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records.
misconceptions: One automatically discovered topology replaces every physical and ownership record.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records.

competencies.statement: Use SLAs, service objectives, wireless surveys, and heat maps with date, method, owner, and limitation context.
misconceptions: A heat map is permanent evidence and needs no measured validation after changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use SLAs, service objectives, wireless surveys, and heat maps with date, method, owner, and limitation context.

competencies.statement: Plan EOL, EOS, patches, operating systems, firmware, licensing, replacement, and secure decommissioning.
misconceptions: End of support means a device stops forwarding traffic immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan EOL, EOS, patches, operating systems, firmware, licensing, replacement, and secure decommissioning.

competencies.statement: Use request, impact, approval, schedule, backup, baseline, golden configuration, rollback, verification, and review controls.
misconceptions: A successful lab change may be copied to production without separate impact analysis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use request, impact, approval, schedule, backup, baseline, golden configuration, rollback, verification, and review controls.

competencies.statement: Use SNMP v2c or v3, MIBs, polling, traps, community or user security, and management scope appropriately.
misconceptions: SNMP traps provide a complete history and remove the need for polling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use SNMP v2c or v3, MIBs, polling, traps, community or user security, and management scope appropriately.

competencies.statement: Combine flow data, packet capture, interface metrics, and baselines to distinguish conversation, content, and anomaly evidence.
misconceptions: Flow records contain complete packet payloads for application diagnosis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine flow data, packet capture, interface metrics, and baselines to distinguish conversation, content, and anomaly evidence.

competencies.statement: Aggregate syslog and application events, correlate in SIEM, and integrate APIs with time, identity, parsing, retention, and access controls.
misconceptions: Centralizing logs guarantees events are complete, correctly parsed, and trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Aggregate syslog and application events, correlate in SIEM, and integrate APIs with time, identity, parsing, retention, and access controls.

competencies.statement: Use scheduled or ad hoc discovery, port mirroring, and traffic, performance, availability, and configuration monitoring.
misconceptions: Port mirroring captures every packet without oversubscription or drop risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scheduled or ad hoc discovery, port mirroring, and traffic, performance, availability, and configuration monitoring.

competencies.statement: Define RPO and RTO from business impact and map them to backup, replication, restoration, and test requirements.
misconceptions: RPO measures how quickly a service must return after failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define RPO and RTO from business impact and map them to backup, replication, restoration, and test requirements.

competencies.statement: Use MTTR and MTBF as population and process indicators without treating averages as individual guarantees.
misconceptions: A higher MTTR indicates a more reliable component.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use MTTR and MTBF as population and process indicators without treating averages as individual guarantees.

competencies.statement: Compare cold, warm, and hot sites by readiness, data, network, staffing, cost, and activation time.
misconceptions: A hot site is instantly current without replication or operational testing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare cold, warm, and hot sites by readiness, data, network, staffing, cost, and activation time.

competencies.statement: Compare active-active and active-passive designs including health checks, state, quorum, load, split brain, and failover.
misconceptions: Active-active design removes every shared dependency and failure mode.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare active-active and active-passive designs including health checks, state, quorum, load, split brain, and failover.

competencies.statement: Run tabletop and validation tests with scenarios, observers, success criteria, rollback, evidence, and remediation owners.
misconceptions: A written disaster-recovery plan is proven once leadership approves it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run tabletop and validation tests with scenarios, observers, success criteria, rollback, evidence, and remediation owners.

competencies.statement: Distinguish confidentiality, integrity, availability, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, likelihood, and impact.
misconceptions: A vulnerability is the same thing as an active attacker exploiting it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish confidentiality, integrity, availability, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, likelihood, and impact.

competencies.statement: Protect data in transit and at rest and explain certificates, PKI trust, self-signed limits, key management, and rotation.
misconceptions: Encryption automatically authenticates the peer and protects key storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect data in transit and at rest and explain certificates, PKI trust, self-signed limits, key management, and rotation.

competencies.statement: Use MFA, SSO, RADIUS, LDAP, SAML, TACACS+, time-based authentication, authorization, RBAC, and least privilege appropriately.
misconceptions: Authentication proves what an identity is allowed to do.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use MFA, SSO, RADIUS, LDAP, SAML, TACACS+, time-based authentication, authorization, RBAC, and least privilege appropriately.

competencies.statement: Apply cameras, locks, geofencing, honeypots, honeynets, audits, data locality, PCI DSS, and GDPR within their actual scope.
misconceptions: Deploying a honeypot prevents attackers from reaching production systems.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply cameras, locks, geofencing, honeypots, honeynets, audits, data locality, PCI DSS, and GDPR within their actual scope.

competencies.statement: Enforce trusted, untrusted, screened, guest, BYOD, IoT, IIoT, SCADA, ICS, and OT boundaries with explicit flows.
misconceptions: Internal segmentation is unnecessary when the internet edge has a firewall.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce trusted, untrusted, screened, guest, BYOD, IoT, IIoT, SCADA, ICS, and OT boundaries with explicit flows.

competencies.statement: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence.
misconceptions: Encryption at the application layer prevents every Layer 2 attack.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain evil twin, rogue AP, DNS poisoning or spoofing, and on-path behavior with detection and user impact.
misconceptions: A familiar SSID proves an access point belongs to the organization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain evil twin, rogue AP, DNS poisoning or spoofing, and on-path behavior with detection and user impact.

competencies.statement: Explain DoS or DDoS, phishing, dumpster diving, shoulder surfing, tailgating, and malware network impact.
misconceptions: Network controls can fully prevent every social-engineering compromise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain DoS or DDoS, phishing, dumpster diving, shoulder surfing, tailgating, and malware network impact.

competencies.statement: Disable unused ports and services, change defaults, use port security, 802.1X, MAC filtering limits, and key management.
misconceptions: MAC filtering provides strong identity because MAC addresses cannot be changed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Disable unused ports and services, change defaults, use port security, 802.1X, MAC filtering limits, and key management.

competencies.statement: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.
misconceptions: An ACL automatically evaluates the most specific rule before earlier rules.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.

competencies.statement: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
misconceptions: A link light proves cabling meets speed and error requirements.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.

competencies.statement: Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
misconceptions: Any CRC counter value proves the cable is currently defective.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.

competencies.statement: Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
misconceptions: A powered endpoint proves the switch has enough PoE budget for all ports.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.

competencies.statement: Diagnose loops, root selection, port roles and states, VLAN assignment, trunking, and ACL direction or order.
misconceptions: A blocked spanning-tree port is always a fault that should be enabled.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose loops, root selection, port roles and states, VLAN assignment, trunking, and ACL direction or order.

competencies.statement: Diagnose route table, default route, gateway, IP, duplicate address, subnet mask, and pool-exhaustion failures.
misconceptions: If the destination route exists, the return path must also exist.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose route table, default route, gateway, IP, duplicate address, subnet mask, and pool-exhaustion failures.

competencies.statement: Distinguish congestion, contention, bottleneck, bandwidth, throughput, and capacity using baselines and path evidence.
misconceptions: A high interface speed guarantees high end-to-end application throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish congestion, contention, bottleneck, bandwidth, throughput, and capacity using baselines and path evidence.

competencies.statement: Measure and relate latency, packet loss, jitter, retransmission, queueing, and application tolerance.
misconceptions: Average latency alone reveals packet loss and jitter behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure and relate latency, packet loss, jitter, retransmission, queueing, and application tolerance.

competencies.statement: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.
misconceptions: Increasing AP transmit power fixes weak coverage without affecting clients or roaming.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.

competencies.statement: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.
misconceptions: A successful ping proves DNS, transport ports, and the application are healthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.

competencies.statement: Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.
misconceptions: A closed or filtered port always proves the destination service is stopped.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.

competencies.statement: Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
misconceptions: An internet speed test measures the capacity of every internal network segment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.

competencies.statement: Choose toner, cable tester, tap, Wi-Fi analyzer, and visual fault locator with correct media, safety, and evidence limits.
misconceptions: A toner certifies cable category, wire map, length, and throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose toner, cable tester, tap, Wi-Fi analyzer, and visual fault locator with correct media, safety, and evidence limits.

competencies.statement: Interpret MAC table, route, interface, configuration, ARP, VLAN, and power command outputs without assuming vendor syntax.
misconceptions: One show command provides enough context to change production configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret MAC table, route, interface, configuration, ARP, VLAN, and power command outputs without assuming vendor syntax.

competencies.statement: Solve performance-based configuration and troubleshooting tasks by preserving requirements, recording state, and verifying all paths.
misconceptions: PBQs reward rapid trial-and-error changes more than planned verification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve performance-based configuration and troubleshooting tasks by preserving requirements, recording state, and verifying all paths.

competencies.statement: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
misconceptions: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.

credential.title: CompTIA Network+ N10-009 Applied Networking Certificate

modules.title: Network Evidence, Safety, and Troubleshooting Method
modules.description: Operate a safe virtual help-desk lab with tickets, diagrams, baselines, commands, packet evidence, and change records. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a reproducible network evidence notebook while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use the N10-009 troubleshooting method before guessing fixes.
Protect production state and user data while gathering evidence.
Communicate symptoms, scope, cause, action, outcome, and prevention clearly.

modules.title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence
modules.description: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a layered packet-flow diagnosis while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use OSI and TCP/IP models as evidence maps rather than protocol mythology.
Trace addressing, encapsulation, PDUs, state, and device responsibility end to end.
Interpret packet evidence without claiming visibility into encrypted or absent layers.

modules.title: Appliances, Functions, Storage, and Cloud Connectivity
modules.description: Design service paths across physical and virtual devices, private cloud networks, public services, and storage. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a labeled hybrid-service path while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare device and application functions instead of memorizing boxes.
Trace policy, availability, storage, and traffic effects across virtual and physical appliances.
Choose cloud models and connectivity from security, latency, scale, and ownership needs.

modules.title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types
modules.description: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a verified protocol and service matrix while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Associate standard ports with purpose, security, and transport behavior.
Trace name, address, session, and application dependencies instead of memorizing isolated numbers.
Distinguish traffic delivery types and tunneling or security protocols.

modules.title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures
modules.description: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a physical media and topology bill of design while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select copper, fiber, wireless, cellular, satellite, and transceivers from measurable constraints.
Match connectors, form factors, standards, and polarity correctly.
Choose topologies and traffic architectures from failure domains and flow patterns.

modules.title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design
modules.description: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify special, public, private, and legacy classful address context.
Calculate network, broadcast, host range, prefix, mask, and capacity accurately.
Allocate VLSM subnets and summarize routes without overlap or wasted assumptions.

modules.title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks
modules.description: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use IPv6 notation, types, scopes, neighbor behavior, and transition choices.
Explain SDN, SD-WAN, VXLAN, ZTA, SASE, and SSE without marketing shortcuts.
Treat network automation as reviewed software with drift and rollback controls.

modules.title: Routing, Selection, Translation, and Redundancy
modules.description: Connect branch, headquarters, cloud, and internet routes while diagnosing asymmetry and failover. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a routed hybrid branch design while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Read and compare routes by prefix, administrative distance, and metric.
Explain static, dynamic, default, translated, and redundant gateway behavior.
Verify forwarding and return paths with tables and packet evidence.

modules.title: VLANs, Trunks, STP, Aggregation, and MTU
modules.description: Segment a campus switch fabric for staff, voice, guest, management, and redundant uplinks. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a verified switched campus configuration plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Configure VLAN membership and tagged links without native-VLAN ambiguity.
Prevent loops and predict spanning-tree port behavior.
Aggregate links and manage speed, duplex, MTU, and SVI dependencies.

modules.title: Wireless Design, Configuration, and Validation
modules.description: Design staff, guest, and point-to-point wireless service across crowded, regulated, and mobile spaces. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a secure wireless survey and deployment plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose bands, channels, widths, antennas, and AP architecture from measured requirements.
Configure identities, encryption, authentication, guest isolation, and roaming.
Validate coverage, capacity, interference, and client behavior rather than signal alone.

modules.title: Facilities, Racks, Cabling, Power, and Environment
modules.description: Plan an MDF, IDFs, racks, patching, power, cooling, security, and maintenance access for a new site. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a facilities-ready network installation package while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Place distribution spaces, racks, panels, pathways, and devices for service and safety.
Calculate power and redundancy without exceeding circuits or PoE budgets.
Control temperature, humidity, airflow, fire, and physical access risks.

modules.title: DHCP, DNS, IPv6 Services, and Time
modules.description: Implement redundant address, name, and time services for clients, infrastructure, and secure operations. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a validated core network-services design while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Configure dynamic addressing and relays with exclusions and reservations.
Build and troubleshoot forward, reverse, authoritative, recursive, and secure DNS behavior.
Select and verify time services required for logs, certificates, and authentication.

modules.title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths
modules.description: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose VPN scope and tunneling behavior from user and routing needs.
Select SSH, GUI, API, console, and jump-host paths with authentication and audit.
Preserve an out-of-band recovery route without bypassing security.

modules.title: Documentation, Change, Lifecycle, and Monitoring
modules.description: Operate a living network source of truth with baselines, telemetry, alerts, configuration, and service ownership. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of an operations and observability runbook while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Maintain diagrams, inventories, IPAM, maps, SLAs, and configuration sources of truth.
Use safe change, lifecycle, backup, patch, and decommissioning processes.
Collect and correlate monitoring evidence without mistaking data volume for observability.

modules.title: Availability, Disaster Recovery, and Validation
modules.description: Define recovery objectives and prove branch, service, configuration, and site recovery under realistic failures. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a tested network continuity plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Calculate and distinguish repair, failure, data-loss, and recovery-time metrics.
Choose sites and active modes from business and technical constraints.
Validate recovery through tabletop and technical exercises.

modules.title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening
modules.description: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Apply CIA, risk, identity, certificates, encryption, and physical controls to network decisions.
Recognize attack evidence without collapsing vulnerability, exploit, threat, and impact.
Implement least privilege, segmentation, NAC, filtering, and hardening with verification.

modules.title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting
modules.description: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of an evidence-backed incident resolution packet while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Diagnose physical and interface counters before replacing devices blindly.
Trace switching, routing, addressing, and service dependencies.
Separate bandwidth, throughput, latency, loss, jitter, congestion, and wireless causes.

modules.title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone
modules.description: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose the least invasive tool that can falsify the current theory.
Interpret host, discovery, capture, cable, wireless, and device command evidence.
Integrate every N10-009 domain in performance-based tasks and a final exam.
